content.content:
- content: 'Assistance-request preparation uses the minimum fields needed to link
    a

    request to the current journey: booking reference, passenger name,

    contact details, flight number, departure airport and time, assistance

    type, and the intended pickup point when applicable.


    A prepared request should record its request mode and retain the evidence

    references used to assemble it. Passport numbers, payment details, and

    unrelated profile or booking fields are not part of the standard request

    payload. These notes describe field structure only; they do not determine

    whether a standard or late-request mode applies.

    '

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Assistance and Accessibility –\
  \ Late assistance bookings
- url: https://www.hial.co.uk/inverness-airport/assistance-accessibility
\
  - source: Highlands and Islands Airports Limited
- domain: hial.co.uk

## Content
\
  
# Late assistance bookings

Inverness Airport states that best efforts will\
  \ be made to accommodate late assistance bookings.

A late request should not\
  \ be treated as a guaranteed pre-arranged service. Where advance information has\
  \ not been provided in time, the assistance available may be limited or delayed\
  \ according to the circumstances at the airport.

"
metadata: {}
title: "Assistance and Accessibility – Late assistance bookings"
url: https://www.hial.co.uk/inverness-airport/assistance-accessibility

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to access help and support | UK Civil Aviation Authority

  - url: https://www.caa.co.uk/air-passengers/assisted-travel/how-to-access-help-and-support/

  - source: UK Civil Aviation Authority

  - domain: caa.co.uk


  ## Content


  # How to access help and support


  ## Requesting assistance


  Passengers who need assisted travel should make the request when booking, or at
  least 48 hours before departure where possible. The request can normally be made
  through the airline, travel agent, or tour operator. Advance notice gives the airport
  and the local assistance provider time to prepare for the passenger''s arrival and
  needs.


  Where assistance is not requested in advance, the passenger may experience delays
  or may not receive all of the support needed for the journey.


  Airlines, travel agents, and tour operators must provide a free method for requesting
  assistance, either during booking or later. Depending on the company, the passenger
  may need to use an assistance link, call a support number, complete a form, or request
  a call-back. Passengers should ask for and retain written confirmation of the assistance
  reservation.


  ## Describing the assistance needed


  Passengers should clearly explain what support they require. Relevant assistance
  can include:


  - transfer from a car park, bus stop, or drop-off point to the terminal;

  - use of an airport wheelchair to reach the gate;

  - help through airport security;

  - assistance with boarding and reaching a seat;

  - seating arrangements connected with the passenger''s needs.


  The airline should also be told about electric wheelchairs, other mobility aids,
  or a medical condition that requires additional planning.


  ## Arriving at the airport


  Passengers who require assistance should allow additional time for check-in and
  should confirm the recommended arrival time before travel.


  Assistance points may be located inside or outside the terminal, including near
  drop-off areas, car parks, railway stations, or bus stops. They normally display
  an assistance or disability-related sign and may provide a telephone or call button
  where the point is not continuously staffed.


  Assistance staff may help the passenger reach a waiting area, check-in or bag drop,
  security, the departure lounge, and the boarding gate. A passenger using a personal
  mobility aid should normally be able to keep using it until the departure gate before
  it is placed in the aircraft hold.


  ## Boarding and the flight


  Travel-assistance staff can help the passenger move through the gate, board by lift,
  ramp, or transfer wheelchair, reach the aircraft seat, and stow cabin baggage where
  required.


  Airline staff are not generally expected to provide personal care such as eating,
  drinking, taking medication, or using the toilet. A passenger who needs that type
  of support may need to travel with someone who can provide it.


  Passengers should discuss onboard accessibility needs with the airline before travel,
  including the availability of an onboard wheelchair and any preferred seating near
  the toilet.


  ## At the destination


  A wheelchair or mobility aid should normally be returned at the arrival gate unless
  there is an exceptional operational reason. Assistance may also continue through
  immigration, customs, baggage reclaim, and the airport exit or onward transport
  points.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How to access help and support | UK Civil Aviation Authority
url: https://www.caa.co.uk/air-passengers/assisted-travel/how-to-access-help-and-support/

args.reason: Submit the user's already-recorded wheelchair assistance request through Inverness Airport's official assistance channel. The request is complete and the user asked to arrange it; because it is 42 hours before departure, submit under late best-efforts handling.

content.content:
Your assistance has been arranged for:

- **Passenger:** Elena Ward
- **Flight:** Loganair LM593
- **Route:** Inverness (INV) to Manchester (MAN)
- **Departure:** 15 July 2026 at 15:00
- **Assistance:** Long-distance wheelchair assistance
- **Pickup point:** Terminal entrance

The request was submitted through **Inverness Airport’s official assistance channel** and accepted.

Because it was submitted **42 hours before departure**, it falls outside the usual **48-hour advance-notice window**. Inverness Airport will make **best efforts**, but the service is not guaranteed to be pre-arranged and could be limited or delayed. The standard airport assistance is the appropriate option; the paid meet-and-assist service is not necessary for the recorded wheelchair assistance request.

Please arrive early and go to the assistance point at the terminal entrance. Official guidance also recommends retaining written confirmation and allowing extra time for check-in and security.
